settings.title: Five Years In, Cleaning for a Local Refuge
settings.excerpt: Our fifth birthday seemed like the moment to give a few of our hours each month to somewhere that needed them.
settings.body:
Sparkhaus is five years old this summer, grown from one person with a car boot of supplies to several teams across the area. It went quicker than any of us expected, and entirely on recommendation rather than advertising.

To mark it, we have committed to a regular free clean each month for a local women's refuge. They do extraordinary work on almost nothing, and a fresh, clean space matters more there than almost anywhere. For families arriving with very little, walking into somewhere that feels cared for is its own small kind of welcome.

How it works

Once a month a team gives the shared spaces a proper going-over, the kitchens, bathrooms, and communal rooms that take the heaviest use. We supply everything ourselves, so it costs the refuge nothing and takes nothing from their own stretched budget.

  • A full clean of the shared spaces every month
  • All products and equipment supplied by us
  • The same vetted team each visit, for consistency and trust

Our teams volunteered for the rota faster than we could write it, which told us everything about the people we work with. Nobody was asked twice. That spirit is the thing we are proudest of after five years, more than the growth or the client list.

To every client who has recommended us to a neighbour over these five years, thank you. That word of mouth is the only reason we grew, and now it is the reason we can give a little of that time back.
